violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. -Nacho cheese is not date marked for open date. -Open jar of alfredo is not date marked. -Taco meat in walk in cooler is not dated.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -White gravy and brown gravy dated 11-11 (believed to be misdated).
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not designed and/or maintained properly. -Grease/residue build up at hood vents and surround.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Some black debris at side interiors of ice machine.--Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Splatter at upper interior of microwave. -Residue on potato cooker used in microwave.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Debris/grease build up below flat top grill. -Grease residue on step stool. -Small chest freezer in dry storage area has frost build up at interior sides. -Upright pizza freezer has frost build up on shelves. -Debris/grease build up below flat top grill. -Grease residue on step stool.

Water Supply. — --Nonpublic water supply was not tested by EPA-certified laboratory annually for nitrate analysis. -Last water sample unknown.--Nonpublic water supply was not tested by EPA-certified laboratory monthly for bacteriological analysis. -Last water sample unknown.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. -Employee (home canned) items stored in refrigerator.
